title: Ready for Anything Day
story: Every October 13, people around the world celebrate International Day for Disaster Risk Reduction by learning how to stay safe when emergencies happen. title: Puffy Pudding That's Not Really Pudding
story: Yorkshire pudding isn't actually pudding at all! On October 13, people across Britain and beyond celebrate this crispy, golden treat that's more like a savory popover. bonus_fact: Eddie Mathews later managed the Atlanta Braves from 1972 to 1974.

title: Rock Star Sammy Hagar's Birthday Bash
story: Red-hot rocker Sammy Hagar burst onto the scene in 1947. Born on October 13 in Salinas, California, he'd grow up to become a legendary vocalist. Hagar's powerful voice led him to fame with his solo hit 'I Can't Drive 55' and as the lead singer of Van Halen from 1985 to 1996. His time with the band produced the smash hit 'Right Now'.

bonus_fact: Sammy Hagar's nickname is 'The Red Rocker' because he often wears red on stage.

title: A Voice That Rocked the Metal World
story: Joey Belladonna burst onto the music scene with a powerful voice that would shake the foundations of heavy metal. Born in Oswego, New York on October 13, 1960, he became the lead singer of the legendary band Anthrax. Belladonna's fierce vocals helped define the thrash metal sound, especially on their hit song 'I Am The Law'.

title: Chicago's Musical Maestro Born
story: Keyboards came alive when Robert Lamm entered the world in Brooklyn, New York. Born on October 13, 1944, Lamm grew up to become a musical powerhouse. title: Jazz Legend's Soulful Journey Begins
story: A musical star was born in Little Rock, Arkansas on October 13, 1940. Farrell Sanders, later known as 'Pharoah' Sanders, would grow up to become a jazz saxophone sensation. title: Jazz Legend Ray Brown's Musical Journey Begins
story: A musical star was born in Pittsburgh on October 13, 1926. Ray Brown, destined to become a jazz giant, entered the world. This talented double bassist would go on to play with greats like Oscar Peterson and Ella Fitzgerald. Brown's deep, rich tones on the bass helped shape the sound of jazz for decades to come.

title: A Champion for Domestic Workers
story: Dorothy Bolden was born in Atlanta, Georgia in 1923. She grew up to become a powerful voice for workers' rights. Bolden founded the National Domestic Worker's Union of America, fighting for fair treatment of housekeepers and nannies. Her work on October 13 and every day helped improve lives and inspire others to stand up for what's right.

story: In a small town in China, a future space explorer took his first breath.
